meaning: n.系铃的公羊,前导,领导者,群众的首领
examples: IBM is considered the bellwether stock on Wall Street.
The Methodist church is viewed as a bellwether of U.S. attitudes in large part.
University campuses are often the bellwether of change.
The bellwether PSEi dropped 3.8%, the biggest single-day decline by percentage since September 2011.
Then, as another bellwether, watch for approval of the economically obvious XL oil pipeline from Canada.
